steps.title: Compare the claims
steps.content: Text 2 argues that professional poetry publication often requires training, practice, and time unavailable to people who work full-time jobs. Text 1 emphasizes Lorde's view that poetry can still be written in spare moments and ordinary places, even by people earning a living another way.

steps.title: Rule out unsupported replies
steps.content: A makes an income claim about poets who publish in major journals, which Text 1 never discusses. C shifts to artists in more financially rewarding forms, and D invents a reason people work full-time. Lorde would point to valuable poetry produced outside Text 2's professional conditions. The answer is B.
